headline: PDD Holdings (PDD) Stock Fair Value Falls After Analysts Cut Targets On Q1 Concerns
summary: PDD Holdings has seen its modeled fair value trimmed from US$148.52 to US$119.85, a cut of about 19% that resets the price target reference point for many investors. This shift lines up with a more cautious tone in recent Street research, where several firms have lowered their targets and flagged concerns around heavier investment, softer monetization and slower order trends. As you read on, you will see how these changing targets feed into the evolving analyst narrative and what to watch...

headline: Citi's overseas retreat advances with sale of Polish business
summary: The megabank has completed the sale of its consumer banking business in Poland. It has now sold 10 retail-focused franchises in certain underperforming, international markets over the past five years.

headline: Zoetis (ZTS) Stock Draws Fresh Price Target Split After Difficult Q1 Update
summary: Zoetis is back in focus as analysts recalibrate their price targets, with Citi cutting its view to US$112 from US$145 while other firms move to US$95 and US$115 after a sharp reset in sentiment. These changes track closely with a Q1 update that some call "difficult" and "worse than it seems," even as bullish voices highlight the company’s premium portfolio and revised 2026 guidance. The following sections explain how to interpret these shifting targets and keep up with the evolving analyst...

headline: ‘Citibank and Amex Have Screwed an 85-Year-Old Widow’: Dave Ramsey on $45,000 in Credit Card Debt With No Assets
summary: On a recent episode of The Ramsey Show, a caller named Michelle from New York described a situation many adult children quietly dread. After her father passed away in July, she discovered her 85-year-old widowed mother had accumulated $45,000 across 5 credit cards, including American Express and two Citibank cards.
